mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off equity selling over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.4pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the 12-month trailing CPI inflation rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real yields rise sufficiently to stabilize equity valuations and generate positive weekly returns.
failure_condition: Positive real yield spreads >1.5pp produce negative 5-day returns in >65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration that suppresses forward earnings expectations and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases over a 4-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes are followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and retail sales during high-inflation regimes signals demand destruction and supply chain contraction, triggering immediate negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint supply-demand decline during high inflation yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.
